Based on the provided evidence, I would like to add another reason to support disagreement with the initial conclusion. While the majority of lymph from the anterior parts of the tongue indeed drains centrally or ventrally, there is significant variation depending on the exact section being referred to. Specifically, the posterior third of the tongue drains directly into the deep cervical lymph nodes rather than following any central or marginal route. Given that "Marginal" refers generally to the outer edges where less centralized drainage typically occurs, specifying 'POSTERIOR' as distinct emphasizes unique characteristics of different sections. Therefore, considering this detailed information about varying patterns specifically mentioning posterior drainage, I now believe the correct answer must account for this specificity.
\boxed{\text{C}}
This boxed letter reflects agreement with Option C - POSTERIOR - acknowledging the distinct nature of posterior tongue lymphatic drainage separate from generalized descriptions potentially misleading if not precisely targeted according to anatomical divisions highlighted here.
